title: senecio bidentatus
intro: Senecio bidentatus, also known as the two-toothed ragwort or bush ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to New Zealand and has been traditionally used for its medicinal and health benefits, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant has been used in herbal preparations to relieve coughs and colds, as well as to treat skin conditions and wounds. In traditional Maori medicine, Senecio bidentatus is often used in teas and infusions to promote overall health and well-being, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity.
